articles.title: Water Company in Trouble for Not Giving People Water
articles.summary: South East Water is a company that gives water to homes in Kent and Sussex. The government regulator, called Ofwat, says the company did a bad job. In 2020 and 2023, many people had no water from their taps. They could not shower, bathe, or flush toilets. This made people feel very stressed and worried. Ofwat wants to fine the company £22 million for these problems. South East Water says it does not agree and is trying to stop the fine in court. The company also had issues in 2023 and 2024, leaving thousands without water. Ofwat found that the company did not plan well for hot or cold weather and did not fix its pipes and reservoirs. Now, Ofwat is checking if the company helped customers properly during these times.

articles.title: New Rules for Asylum Seekers Who Break the Law
articles.summary: The UK government is making new rules for asylum seekers, who are people asking for safety in another country. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood will announce that if asylum seekers break the law or work illegally, they will lose their government support, like housing and money. This is to make the UK less attractive for illegal migrants and reduce small boat crossings in the English Channel. The changes will start in June and are inspired by Denmark's system. Some politicians support this, while others, like the Green Party, criticize it. Mahmood says these rules are compassionate but controlled to help those who truly need it.

articles.title: Elon Musk Goes to Court Over Twitter Deal
articles.summary: Elon Musk, a very rich man who owns companies like Tesla and SpaceX, went to court in San Francisco. He was defending himself against claims that he tricked people who owned Twitter stock. Musk had agreed to buy Twitter for $44 billion but later tried to back out, saying Twitter had too many fake accounts called bots. The lawsuit says Musk made false statements that hurt Twitter's stock price. In court, Musk wore a black suit and tie and said he was upset with Twitter's board because he felt they lied to him. He eventually bought Twitter anyway after his lawyers said he might lose in court. This trial is about whether Musk broke laws while trying to get a better deal.

articles.title: Sea Levels Are Higher Than We Thought, Putting More People in Danger
articles.summary: A new study found that sea levels are higher than scientists previously believed, which means more people are at risk from flooding. Researchers looked at many studies and discovered that about 90% of them underestimated how high coastal waters are by about 1 foot on average. This mistake happens because measuring sea and land heights uses different methods, and factors like waves and currents are often ignored. The problem is worse in places like the Global South, Pacific, and Southeast Asia. If sea levels rise by 3 feet by the end of the century, as some predict, up to 37% more land could be flooded, threatening 77 to 132 million more people. This makes planning for climate change harder and more expensive.

articles.title: World Cup Challenges: Iran War and Mexico Violence
articles.summary: The 2026 World Cup is a big soccer tournament starting in 100 days, hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada. It faces problems like a war involving Iran and violence from cartels in Mexico. Iran is a team that qualified to play, but it might not come to the U.S. because of the war. Mexico has safety issues in one host city. Organizers are also dealing with high ticket prices and smaller fan events. The World Cup will have 48 teams, more than before, and starts with Mexico vs. South Africa in Mexico City. Politics often affect sports events, and this time, tensions with Iran and other countries add challenges.

articles.title: A New Leader for the Money Bank
articles.summary: The White House has chosen Kevin Warsh to be the next leader of the Federal Reserve, which is like the big bank for the United States. He used to work there before. The Senate, a group in the government, needs to say yes to this choice. But one senator, Thom Tillis, says no because there is a problem with the current leader, Jerome Powell. Kevin Warsh has said the Federal Reserve made some mistakes with money rules, like keeping interest rates too low, which made prices go up fast. Now, he wants lower rates because he thinks new technology called artificial intelligence will help the economy grow without making prices rise. Other leaders at the Federal Reserve do not all agree with him. The Senate will talk about this and vote to decide if Kevin Warsh gets the job.
